title: Tangy Crumbly Peruvian Quinoa Curry
description: A vegan Peruvian-inspired quinoa curry that balances tangy lime, smoky bhuna spices, and a crumbly vegan feta topping for a satisfying meal prep dish.

ingredients.name: ground cumin
ingredients.notes: dry roast in a dry skillet for 30 seconds

ingredients.name: ground coriander
ingredients.notes: dry roast in a dry skillet for 30 seconds

ingredients.name: vegan feta crumble
ingredients.notes: toss into the curry just before serving

instructions: Rinse quinoa under cold water until the water runs clear. Drain.
In a small pan, dry roast cumin, coriander, and smoked paprika on medium heat until fragrant, about 30 seconds. Set aside.
Combine quinoa and vegetable broth in a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until quinoa is tender and liquid is absorbed.
Meanwhile, heat a large skillet over medium heat and add a splash of oil. Sauté sweet potato, carrots, and red bell pepper for 3-4 minutes.
Add the roasted spices, chili powder, turmeric, salt, and black pepper to the vegetables; stir to coat and cook for 1 minute.
Pour in coconut milk, add cherry tomatoes, and bring to a gentle simmer. Cook for another 5 minutes until vegetables are tender and sauce slightly thickens.
Fold in the cooked quinoa, kale, and lime juice. Stir until kale wilts and everything is heated through, about 2 minutes.
Remove from heat, discard the bay leaf, and transfer the curry to meal prep containers. Sprinkle vegan feta crumble over each portion.
Let cool to room temperature, then cover and refrigerate up to 4 days. Reheat in the microwave or on the stovetop before serving.
